name: From the Santarém Plateau to the Tejo River Trail (PR2)
name_original: Do Planalto de Santarém ao Rio Tejo (PR 2) — percurso circular
description: This easy 4.4-mile (7.0 km) hiking route offers a blend of Santarém's historic cityscapes and panoramic views of the Tejo River.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around Santarém (São Salvador) traverse a landscape shaped by the Tagus and Zêzere rivers, offering diverse environments. The region features karstic formations, including limestone ridges and dolines, particularly within the Parque Natural da Serras de Aire e Candeeiros. Hikers can explore riverine zones with ash and willow trees, Mediterranean vegetation like cork and holm oak woods, and areas with historical significance. Best family friendly hiking trails around Santarém (São Salvador) The most popular family friendly hiking route is From the Santarém Plateau to the Tejo River Trail (PR2), a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete. This easy route blends historic cityscapes with panoramic views of the Tagus River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sá da Bandeira Square – Jardim das Portas do Sol loop from Santarém, an easy 4.3 miles (7.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Tagus floodplain from the Portas do Sol lookout. Local hikers also love the Jardim das Portas do Sol – Igreja de São João de Alporão loop from Santarém, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through urban and scenic areas, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Santarém (São Salvador) is defined by riverine environments, karstic landscapes, and Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on family friendly hiking trails. The routes in Santarém (São Salvador) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Santarém (São Salvador)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: There are 9 family-friendly hiking trails around Santarém (São Salvador) featured in this guide. These routes are specifically chosen for their manageable distances and gentle paths, making them perfect for families with children.

Q: What kind of landscapes can we expect on family hikes in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: The region offers a diverse range of landscapes, from scenic riverine environments along the Tagus and Zêzere rivers to the unique karstic formations of the Parque Natural da Serras de Aire e Candeeiros. You'll find lush forests, limestone ridges, and Mediterranean vegetation, providing varied and engaging scenery for family adventures.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, several family-friendly routes are circular, offering a convenient way to explore without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32151108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sá da Bandeira Square – Jardim das Portas do Sol loop from Santarém&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that takes you through some of the city's charming areas.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along these trails?
A: Many trails offer stunning views and natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5552902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardim das Portas do Sol&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent viewpoint within Santarém itself, offering sweeping panoramic views of the Tagus River. Further afield, the region is known for its karstic terrain and impressive waterfalls like Penedo Furado, though these might be on more adventurous routes not covered in this family-specific guide.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family hiking in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: The komoot community highly rates the family-friendly trails in Santarém (São Salvador), with an average score of 4.8 stars. Reviewers often praise the accessibility of the paths, the beautiful views, and the opportunity to combine nature with cultural exploration, making it a rewarding experience for all ages.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see on these family hikes?
A: Absolutely! Santarém is rich in history. On routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2259903655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Santarém Plateau to the Tejo River Trail (PR2)&lt;/a&gt;, you can blend historic cityscapes with natural beauty. You might encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5554761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Santarém&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553906" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sá da Bandeira Square&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past.

Q: What is the best time of year for family hikes in Santarém (São Salvador)?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for family hiking in Santarém (São Salvador). The weather is mild, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be quite hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this period. Winter offers cooler temperatures, but some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any easy routes that offer good views of the Tagus River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2259903655" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Santarém Plateau to the Tejo River Trail (PR2)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 7 km route that provides panoramic views of the Tagus River floodplain, especially from the Portas do Sol lookout. It's a great way to experience the river's beauty with the family.

Q: What kind of terrain should we expect on these family-friendly trails?
A: The family-friendly trails in this guide are generally characterized by gentle paths and manageable distances. While some may involve slight inclines or varied surfaces typical of natural parks, they are selected to be accessible for children and offer a comfortable hiking experience. You'll often find well-maintained paths through forests, along riverbanks, and within urban parks.

Q: Are there any unique attractions nearby that are suitable for children?
A: Beyond the trails, the broader Santarém region offers unique attractions. For instance, the Monumento Natural das Pegadas de Dinossáurios, located between Ourém and Torres Novas, features well-preserved dinosaur footprints, which can be a fascinating and educational experience for children.

Q: Can we find places to rest or have a picnic along the family routes?
A: Many of the routes, especially those passing through urban parks or scenic viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5552902" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardim das Portas do Sol&lt;/a&gt;, offer benches and open spaces perfect for a rest or a family picnic. It's always a good idea to pack snacks and water, especially for longer walks.
tips: An interesting and recommended starting point. Good views and overview of the city, the surrounding area, and the course of the Tagus River. A pavilion and small restaurant are available for refreshments and are also usable.

Located on Rua 1o de Novembro, in the heart of the historic center of Santarém, this work of art was inaugurated on August 26, 2022. It is a six meter tall figure made from recycled rubbish by the artist Bordalo II. Very unique and beautiful.
A lot of renovation work is currently being carried out in the city, and it is sometimes very difficult to get to some of the buildings.
From here you have a great view of the Tejo. There is also easy parking here, even for free.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins in the Jardim da República in Santarém. You'll find parking and other amenities available in this area, making it a convenient starting point for your hike.

faqs.question: Is the From the Santarém Plateau to the Tejo River Trail (PR2) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through urban areas and potentially near historical sites, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of other hikers and local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures. These seasons are ideal for exploring both the historical cityscapes and the natural riverine sections without extreme heat or cold.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll navigate narrow, winding streets in Santarém's historic center, descend towards the Ribeira de Santarém on an old asphalted road, and then follow the river margin. The ascent back to the plateau includes a steep slope with a footpath and sections of medieval paving stones, which can be uneven and require careful footing.

faqs.question: What historical and natural sights will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of history and nature. You'll pass by significant historical sites like the Convento de São Francisco, the Church of Santa Clara, and the Church of Santa Cruz. Natural highlights include a gallery of riparian trees along the river and panoramic views from the plateau, especially from the Jardim das Portas do Sol and Miradouro do Largo do Barão, offering expansive vistas over the Tejo River and surrounding fields. Don't miss the Hoopoe Mural by Bordalo II.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: While the Komoot tour data suggests approximately 1 hour and 58 minutes, typical hiking guides indicate it takes around 3 hours to complete the 6.0 to 6.2-kilometer circular route, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the sights.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered manageable for most hikers, including families. However, be aware that it includes steep slopes and sections with uneven medieval paving stones, which might be challenging for very young children or those with strollers. Older children who are comfortable with varied terrain should enjoy the historical and natural exploration.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route align with other well-known paths. Notably, it covers 100% of the 'Do planalto de Santarém ao Rio Tejo' trail, and also intersects with parts of the 'Caminho Português Central' (66%), 'Caminho de Tejo' (18%), and 'Caminhos de Fátima' (18%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or tips for navigating the trail?
faqs.answer: The main challenge lies in the varied terrain, particularly the steep ascent back to the plateau and the uneven medieval paving stones. We recommend wearing sturdy footwear with good ankle support. Starting at Jardim da República allows easy access to amenities before you begin. The trail is a circular route, offering a continuous journey through Santarém's highlights.

faqs.question: Are there facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail starts in the Jardim da República area, which is well-equipped with amenities. You'll find parking, and the proximity to Santarém's city center means there are numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops available, including the municipal market near the starting point.
